back_translation: And Moses said to the sons of Israel, according to all that the LORD commanded Moses.
And Moses spoke to the heads of the tribes for the sons of Israel, saying: 'This is the word which the LORD has commanded.'
'A man, when he vows a vow to the LORD or swears an oath to bind a binding-pledge on his soul, he shall not profane his word; according to all that comes out from his mouth he shall do.'
'And a woman, when she vows a vow to the LORD and binds a binding-pledge in her father's house, in her youth.'

'And her father hears her vow and her binding-pledge which she bound on her soul, and her father is silent toward her — and all her vows shall stand, and every binding-pledge which she bound on her soul shall stand.'

'And if her father restrains her on the day of his hearing — all her vows and her binding-pledges which she bound on her soul shall not stand; and the LORD shall forgive her, because her father restrained her.'
'And if she becomes a man's, and her vows are upon her, or the utterance of her lips by which she bound her soul.'
'And her husband hears in the day of his hearing, and is silent toward her — and her vows shall stand, and her binding-pledges which she bound on her soul shall stand.'
'But if in the day her husband hears, he restrains her, and he breaks her vow which is on her and the utterance of her lips which she bound on her soul — and the LORD shall forgive her.'
'And the vow of a widow and a divorced woman — everything she bound on her soul shall stand on her.'
'And if in her husband's house she vowed, or she bound a binding-pledge on her soul with an oath.'
'And her husband hears and is silent toward her, he did not restrain her — and all her vows shall stand, and every binding-pledge which she bound on her soul shall stand.'

'And if breaking her husband breaks them in the day of his hearing, all that came out from her lips for her vows and for the binding-pledge of her soul shall not stand; her husband has broken them, and the LORD shall forgive her.'
'Every vow and every oath of binding-pledge to afflict a soul, her husband shall establish it, and her husband shall break it.'

'But if being silent her husband is silent toward her from day to day, then he establishes all her vows or all her binding-pledges which are on her; he has established them, because he was silent toward her in the day of his hearing.'
'And if breaking he breaks them after his hearing, then he shall bear her iniquity.'
These are the statutes which the LORD commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter in her youth in her father's house.
